Astronomical Observatory of Japan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}

'''National Astronomical Observatory of Japan Mitaka Campus''' (国立天文台三鷹キャンパス) is the headquarters of the [[National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]] (NAOJ), part of the [[National Institutes of Natural Sciences]], located in [[Mitaka]], [[Tokyo]]. The headquarters of the [[Astronomical Society of Japan]] is also located on the campus.
Additionally, the [[Research Center for Astronomy, University of Tokyo]] is situated on this campus. Although it operates as a separate organization, it maintains close relationships with NAOJ through personnel exchanges and collaborative research.
== Facilities ==
=== First Equatorial Room === [[File:The 20-cm Telescope Dome in NAOJ Mitaka Campus-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|First Equatorial Room]] Constructed in [[1921]], a 20cm equatorial telescope manufactured by [[Carl Zeiss AG|Carl Zeiss]] was installed in 1927.<ref name="20cmts">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/20cm-telescope-dome.html|title=First Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The structure is a two-story reinforced concrete building with a floor area of 50 square meters.<ref name="bunka-daiichi">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139891|title=National Astronomical Observatory First Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|Agency for Cultural Affairs]]}}</ref> It was designed by the construction division of [[Tokyo Imperial University]].<ref name="bunka-daiichi" />
The equatorial telescope has a focal length of 359cm and operates using a weight-driven clock mechanism to adjust its speed. The dome is notable for being made of wood.
From [[1938]] to 1999, the telescope was used for solar sunspot observation.<ref name="20cmts" /> The room was registered as a [[Registered Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Registered Tangible Cultural Property]] in 2002.<ref name="20cmts" /><ref name="bunka-daiichi" />
=== Large Equatorial Room === [[File:NAOJ Mitaka Campus 04.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Large Equatorial Room]] Constructed in 1926 and equi11ed with a 65cm refracting telescope in 1929, manufactured by Carl Zeiss.<ref name="hmuseum">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/observatory-history-museum.html|title=Astronomical Observatory History Museum - Large Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The reinforced concrete building has a floor area of 238 square meters.<ref name="bunka-daisekidougi">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187350|title=National Astronomical Observatory Large Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The telescope has a focal length of 1021cm. Due to the large height changes required for observations, the entire floor was designed to rise and fall (range: 360cm) but was fixed in 2000.<ref name="hmuseum" />
The telescope was primarily used for star position observations but ceased operations due to aging. The room, with its wooden dome, was converted into the "Astronomical Observatory History Museum" in 2001 and registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 2002.<ref name="hmuseum" /><ref name="bunka-daisekidougi" />
=== 50cm Public Telescope === A 50cm [[Cassegrain reflector]] telescope built in 1995 by Mitaka Optical Instruments.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/50cm-telescope.html|title=50cm Public Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> It has been in use for public observation sessions twice a month since April 1996.
**Specifications:** - Aperture: 510mm (effective aperture: 500mm) - Focal length: 6030mm - Mount: Fork-type equatorial mount (automated) - Visual limiting magnitude: 16 - CCD limiting magnitude: 21
=== Solar Tower Telescope === [[File:Solar Tower Telescope Einstein tower Mitaka NAOJ 2010.jpg|thumb|180px|Einstein Tower]] Constructed in 1930, it is a reinforced concrete building with five above-ground and one underground floor.<ref name="stower">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/solar-tower-telescope.html|title=Solar Tower Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The facility is modeled after the "Einstein Tower" in [[Potsdam]], Germany.<ref name="stower" /> Registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 1998.<ref name="bunka-taiyoutou">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/147175|title=National Astronomical Observatory Solar Spectroheliograph Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
**Additional Facilities:** - [[Meridian Circles]] - [[TAMA300]] gravitational wave experiment facility - 4D2U Dome Theater - Visitor access and public lectures
== Location == - Address: 2-21-1 Osawa, Mitaka, Tokyo, Japan - Accessibility: By bus from [[Musashi-Sakai Station]] or [[Chofu Station]]
== See Also == * [[National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]] * [[Ishigakijima Astronomical Observatory]] * [[Subaru Telescope]]
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == - [http://www.nao.ac.jp National Astronomical Observatory of Japan] - [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00000565 Cultural Heritage Database – Solar Tower Telescope]

'''National Astronomical Observatory of Japan Mitaka Campus''' (国立天文台三鷹キャンパス) is the headquarters of the [[National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]] (NAOJ), part of the [[National Institutes of Natural Sciences]], located in [[Mitaka]], [[Tokyo]]. The headquarters of the [[Astronomical Society of Japan]] is also located on the campus.
Additionally, the [[Research Center for Astronomy, University of Tokyo]] is situated on this campus. Although it operates as a separate organization, it maintains close relationships with NAOJ through personnel exchanges and collaborative research.
== Facilities ==
=== First Equatorial Room === [[File:The 20-cm Telescope Dome in NAOJ Mitaka Campus-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|First Equatorial Room]] Constructed in [[1921]], a 20cm equatorial telescope manufactured by [[Carl Zeiss AG|Carl Zeiss]] was installed in 1927.<ref name="20cmts">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/20cm-telescope-dome.html|title=First Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The structure is a two-story reinforced concrete building with a floor area of 50 square meters.<ref name="bunka-daiichi">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139891|title=National Astronomical Observatory First Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|Agency for Cultural Affairs]]}}</ref> It was designed by the construction division of [[Tokyo Imperial University]].<ref name="bunka-daiichi" />
The equatorial telescope has a focal length of 359cm and operates using a weight-driven clock mechanism to adjust its speed. The dome is notable for being made of wood.
From [[1938]] to 1999, the telescope was used for solar sunspot observation.<ref name="20cmts" /> The room was registered as a [[Registered Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Registered Tangible Cultural Property]] in 2002.<ref name="20cmts" /><ref name="bunka-daiichi" />
=== Large Equatorial Room === [[File:NAOJ Mitaka Campus 04.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Large Equatorial Room]] Constructed in 1926 and equi11ed with a 65cm refracting telescope in 1929, manufactured by Carl Zeiss.<ref name="hmuseum">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/observatory-history-museum.html|title=Astronomical Observatory History Museum - Large Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The reinforced concrete building has a floor area of 238 square meters.<ref name="bunka-daisekidougi">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187350|title=National Astronomical Observatory Large Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The telescope has a focal length of 1021cm. Due to the large height changes required for observations, the entire floor was designed to rise and fall (range: 360cm) but was fixed in 2000.<ref name="hmuseum" />
The telescope was primarily used for star position observations but ceased operations due to aging. The room, with its wooden dome, was converted into the "Astronomical Observatory History Museum" in 2001 and registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 2002.<ref name="hmuseum" /><ref name="bunka-daisekidougi" />
=== 50cm Public Telescope === A 50cm [[Cassegrain reflector]] telescope built in 1995 by Mitaka Optical Instruments.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/50cm-telescope.html|title=50cm Public Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> It has been in use for public observation sessions twice a month since April 1996.
**Specifications:** - Aperture: 510mm (effective aperture: 500mm) - Focal length: 6030mm - Mount: Fork-type equatorial mount (automated) - Visual limiting magnitude: 16 - CCD limiting magnitude: 21
=== Solar Tower Telescope === [[File:Solar Tower Telescope Einstein tower Mitaka NAOJ 2010.jpg|thumb|180px|Einstein Tower]] Constructed in 1930, it is a reinforced concrete building with five above-ground and one underground floor.<ref name="stower">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/solar-tower-telescope.html|title=Solar Tower Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The facility is modeled after the "Einstein Tower" in [[Potsdam]], Germany.<ref name="stower" /> Registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 1998.<ref name="bunka-taiyoutou">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/147175|title=National Astronomical Observatory Solar Spectroheliograph Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
**Additional Facilities:** - [[Meridian Circles]] - [[TAMA300]] gravitational wave experiment facility - 4D2U Dome Theater - Visitor access and public lectures
== Location == - Address: 2-21-1 Osawa, Mitaka, Tokyo, Japan - Accessibility: By bus from [[Musashi-Sakai Station]] or [[Chofu Station]]

**Additional Facilities:** - [[Meridian Circles]] - [[TAMA300]] gravitational wave experiment facility - 4D2U Dome Theater - Visitor access and public lectures
== Location == - Address: 2-21-1 Osawa, Mitaka, Tokyo, Japan - Accessibility: By bus from [[Musashi-Sakai Station]] or [[Chofu Station]]
== See Also == * [[National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]] * [[Ishigakijima Astronomical Observatory]] * [[Subaru Telescope]]
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == - [http://www.nao.ac.jp National Astronomical Observatory of Japan] - [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00000565 Cultural Heritage Database – Solar Tower Telescope]

revision.text:
'''National Astronomical Observatory of Japan Mitaka Campus''' (国立天文台三鷹キャンパス) is the headquarters of the [[National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]] (NAOJ), part of the [[National Institutes of Natural Sciences]], located in [[Mitaka]], [[Tokyo]]. The headquarters of the [[Astronomical Society of Japan]] is also located on the campus.
Additionally, the [[Research Center for Astronomy, University of Tokyo]] is situated on this campus. Although it operates as a separate organization, it maintains close relationships with NAOJ through personnel exchanges and collaborative research.
== Facilities ==
=== First Equatorial Room === [[File:The 20-cm Telescope Dome in NAOJ Mitaka Campus-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|First Equatorial Room]] Constructed in [[1921]], a 20cm equatorial telescope manufactured by [[Carl Zeiss AG|Carl Zeiss]] was installed in 1927.<ref name="20cmts">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/20cm-telescope-dome.html|title=First Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The structure is a two-story reinforced concrete building with a floor area of 50 square meters.<ref name="bunka-daiichi">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139891|title=National Astronomical Observatory First Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|Agency for Cultural Affairs]]}}</ref> It was designed by the construction division of [[Tokyo Imperial University]].<ref name="bunka-daiichi" />
The equatorial telescope has a focal length of 359cm and operates using a weight-driven clock mechanism to adjust its speed. The dome is notable for being made of wood.
From [[1938]] to 1999, the telescope was used for solar sunspot observation.<ref name="20cmts" /> The room was registered as a [[Registered Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Registered Tangible Cultural Property]] in 2002.<ref name="20cmts" /><ref name="bunka-daiichi" />
=== Large Equatorial Room === [[File:NAOJ Mitaka Campus 04.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Large Equatorial Room]] Constructed in 1926 and equi11ed with a 65cm refracting telescope in 1929, manufactured by Carl Zeiss.<ref name="hmuseum">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/observatory-history-museum.html|title=Astronomical Observatory History Museum - Large Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The reinforced concrete building has a floor area of 238 square meters.<ref name="bunka-daisekidougi">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187350|title=National Astronomical Observatory Large Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The telescope has a focal length of 1021cm. Due to the large height changes required for observations, the entire floor was designed to rise and fall (range: 360cm) but was fixed in 2000.<ref name="hmuseum" />
The telescope was primarily used for star position observations but ceased operations due to aging. The room, with its wooden dome, was converted into the "Astronomical Observatory History Museum" in 2001 and registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 2002.<ref name="hmuseum" /><ref name="bunka-daisekidougi" />
=== 50cm Public Telescope === A 50cm [[Cassegrain reflector]] telescope built in 1995 by Mitaka Optical Instruments.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/50cm-telescope.html|title=50cm Public Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> It has been in use for public observation sessions twice a month since April 1996.
**Specifications:** - Aperture: 510mm (effective aperture: 500mm) - Focal length: 6030mm - Mount: Fork-type equatorial mount (automated) - Visual limiting magnitude: 16 - CCD limiting magnitude: 21
=== Solar Tower Telescope === [[File:Solar Tower Telescope Einstein tower Mitaka NAOJ 2010.jpg|thumb|180px|Einstein Tower]] Constructed in 1930, it is a reinforced concrete building with five above-ground and one underground floor.<ref name="stower">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/solar-tower-telescope.html|title=Solar Tower Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The facility is modeled after the "Einstein Tower" in [[Potsdam]], Germany.<ref name="stower" /> Registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 1998.<ref name="bunka-taiyoutou">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/147175|title=National Astronomical Observatory Solar Spectroheliograph Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
**Additional Facilities:** - [[Meridian Circles]] - [[TAMA300]] gravitational wave experiment facility - 4D2U Dome Theater - Visitor access and public lectures
== Location == - Address: 2-21-1 Osawa, Mitaka, Tokyo, Japan - Accessibility: By bus from [[Musashi-Sakai Station]] or [[Chofu Station]]
== See Also == * [[National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]] * [[Ishigakijima Astronomical Observatory]] * [[Subaru Telescope]]
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == - [http://www.nao.ac.jp National Astronomical Observatory of Japan] - [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00000565 Cultural Heritage Database – Solar Tower Telescope]

'''National Astronomical Observatory of Japan Mitaka Campus''' (国立天文台三鷹キャンパス) is the headquarters of the [[National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]] (NAOJ), part of the [[National Institutes of Natural Sciences]], located in [[Mitaka]], [[Tokyo]]. The headquarters of the [[Astronomical Society of Japan]] is also located on the campus.
Additionally, the [[Research Center for Astronomy, University of Tokyo]] is situated on this campus. Although it operates as a separate organization, it maintains close relationships with NAOJ through personnel exchanges and collaborative research.
== Facilities ==
=== First Equatorial Room === [[File:The 20-cm Telescope Dome in NAOJ Mitaka Campus-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|First Equatorial Room]] Constructed in [[1921]], a 20cm equatorial telescope manufactured by [[Carl Zeiss AG|Carl Zeiss]] was installed in 1927.<ref name="20cmts">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/20cm-telescope-dome.html|title=First Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The structure is a two-story reinforced concrete building with a floor area of 50 square meters.<ref name="bunka-daiichi">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139891|title=National Astronomical Observatory First Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|Agency for Cultural Affairs]]}}</ref> It was designed by the construction division of [[Tokyo Imperial University]].<ref name="bunka-daiichi" />
The equatorial telescope has a focal length of 359cm and operates using a weight-driven clock mechanism to adjust its speed. The dome is notable for being made of wood.
From [[1938]] to 1999, the telescope was used for solar sunspot observation.<ref name="20cmts" /> The room was registered as a [[Registered Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Registered Tangible Cultural Property]] in 2002.<ref name="20cmts" /><ref name="bunka-daiichi" />
=== Large Equatorial Room === [[File:NAOJ Mitaka Campus 04.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Large Equatorial Room]] Constructed in 1926 and equi11ed with a 65cm refracting telescope in 1929, manufactured by Carl Zeiss.<ref name="hmuseum">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/observatory-history-museum.html|title=Astronomical Observatory History Museum - Large Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The reinforced concrete building has a floor area of 238 square meters.<ref name="bunka-daisekidougi">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187350|title=National Astronomical Observatory Large Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The telescope has a focal length of 1021cm. Due to the large height changes required for observations, the entire floor was designed to rise and fall (range: 360cm) but was fixed in 2000.<ref name="hmuseum" />
The telescope was primarily used for star position observations but ceased operations due to aging. The room, with its wooden dome, was converted into the "Astronomical Observatory History Museum" in 2001 and registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 2002.<ref name="hmuseum" /><ref name="bunka-daisekidougi" />
=== 50cm Public Telescope === A 50cm [[Cassegrain reflector]] telescope built in 1995 by Mitaka Optical Instruments.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/50cm-telescope.html|title=50cm Public Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> It has been in use for public observation sessions twice a month since April 1996.
**Specifications:** - Aperture: 510mm (effective aperture: 500mm) - Focal length: 6030mm - Mount: Fork-type equatorial mount (automated) - Visual limiting magnitude: 16 - CCD limiting magnitude: 21
=== Solar Tower Telescope === [[File:Solar Tower Telescope Einstein tower Mitaka NAOJ 2010.jpg|thumb|180px|Einstein Tower]] Constructed in 1930, it is a reinforced concrete building with five above-ground and one underground floor.<ref name="stower">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/solar-tower-telescope.html|title=Solar Tower Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The facility is modeled after the "Einstein Tower" in [[Potsdam]], Germany.<ref name="stower" /> Registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 1998.<ref name="bunka-taiyoutou">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/147175|title=National Astronomical Observatory Solar Spectroheliograph Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
**Additional Facilities:** - [[Meridian Circles]] - [[TAMA300]] gravitational wave experiment facility - 4D2U Dome Theater - Visitor access and public lectures
== Location == - Address: 2-21-1 Osawa, Mitaka, Tokyo, Japan - Accessibility: By bus from [[Musashi-Sakai Station]] or [[Chofu Station]]
== See Also == * [[National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]] * [[Ishigakijima Astronomical Observatory]] * [[Subaru Telescope]]

'''National Astronomical Observatory of Japan Mitaka Campus''' (国立天文台三鷹キャンパス) is the headquarters of the [[National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]] (NAOJ), part of the [[National Institutes of Natural Sciences]], located in [[Mitaka]], [[Tokyo]]. The headquarters of the [[Astronomical Society of Japan]] is also located on the campus.
Additionally, the [[Research Center for Astronomy, University of Tokyo]] is situated on this campus. Although it operates as a separate organization, it maintains close relationships with NAOJ through personnel exchanges and collaborative research.
== Facilities ==
=== First Equatorial Room === [[File:The 20-cm Telescope Dome in NAOJ Mitaka Campus-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|First Equatorial Room]] Constructed in [[1921]], a 20cm equatorial telescope manufactured by [[Carl Zeiss AG|Carl Zeiss]] was installed in 1927.<ref name="20cmts">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/20cm-telescope-dome.html|title=First Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The structure is a two-story reinforced concrete building with a floor area of 50 square meters.<ref name="bunka-daiichi">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139891|title=National Astronomical Observatory First Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|Agency for Cultural Affairs]]}}</ref> It was designed by the construction division of [[Tokyo Imperial University]].<ref name="bunka-daiichi" />
The equatorial telescope has a focal length of 359cm and operates using a weight-driven clock mechanism to adjust its speed. The dome is notable for being made of wood.
From [[1938]] to 1999, the telescope was used for solar sunspot observation.<ref name="20cmts" /> The room was registered as a [[Registered Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Registered Tangible Cultural Property]] in 2002.<ref name="20cmts" /><ref name="bunka-daiichi" />
=== Large Equatorial Room === [[File:NAOJ Mitaka Campus 04.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Large Equatorial Room]] Constructed in 1926 and equi11ed with a 65cm refracting telescope in 1929, manufactured by Carl Zeiss.<ref name="hmuseum">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/observatory-history-museum.html|title=Astronomical Observatory History Museum - Large Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The reinforced concrete building has a floor area of 238 square meters.<ref name="bunka-daisekidougi">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187350|title=National Astronomical Observatory Large Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The telescope has a focal length of 1021cm. Due to the large height changes required for observations, the entire floor was designed to rise and fall (range: 360cm) but was fixed in 2000.<ref name="hmuseum" />
The telescope was primarily used for star position observations but ceased operations due to aging. The room, with its wooden dome, was converted into the "Astronomical Observatory History Museum" in 2001 and registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 2002.<ref name="hmuseum" /><ref name="bunka-daisekidougi" />
=== 50cm Public Telescope === A 50cm [[Cassegrain reflector]] telescope built in 1995 by Mitaka Optical Instruments.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/50cm-telescope.html|title=50cm Public Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> It has been in use for public observation sessions twice a month since April 1996.
**Specifications:** - Aperture: 510mm (effective aperture: 500mm) - Focal length: 6030mm - Mount: Fork-type equatorial mount (automated) - Visual limiting magnitude: 16 - CCD limiting magnitude: 21
=== Solar Tower Telescope === [[File:Solar Tower Telescope Einstein tower Mitaka NAOJ 2010.jpg|thumb|180px|Einstein Tower]] Constructed in 1930, it is a reinforced concrete building with five above-ground and one underground floor.<ref name="stower">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/solar-tower-telescope.html|title=Solar Tower Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The facility is modeled after the "Einstein Tower" in [[Potsdam]], Germany.<ref name="stower" /> Registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 1998.<ref name="bunka-taiyoutou">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/147175|title=National Astronomical Observatory Solar Spectroheliograph Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
**Additional Facilities:** - [[Meridian Circles]] - [[TAMA300]] gravitational wave experiment facility - 4D2U Dome Theater - Visitor access and public lectures
== Location == - Address: 2-21-1 Osawa, Mitaka, Tokyo, Japan - Accessibility: By bus from [[Musashi-Sakai Station]] or [[Chofu Station]]
== See Also == * [[National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]] * [[Ishigakijima Astronomical Observatory]] * [[Subaru Telescope]]

'''National Astronomical Observatory of Japan Mitaka Campus''' (国立天文台三鷹キャンパス) is the headquarters of the [[National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]] (NAOJ), part of the [[National Institutes of Natural Sciences]], located in [[Mitaka]], [[Tokyo]]. The headquarters of the [[Astronomical Society of Japan]] is also located on the campus.
Additionally, the [[Research Center for Astronomy, University of Tokyo]] is situated on this campus. Although it operates as a separate organization, it maintains close relationships with NAOJ through personnel exchanges and collaborative research.
== Facilities ==
=== First Equatorial Room === [[File:The 20-cm Telescope Dome in NAOJ Mitaka Campus-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|First Equatorial Room]] Constructed in [[1921]], a 20cm equatorial telescope manufactured by [[Carl Zeiss AG|Carl Zeiss]] was installed in 1927.<ref name="20cmts">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/20cm-telescope-dome.html|title=First Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The structure is a two-story reinforced concrete building with a floor area of 50 square meters.<ref name="bunka-daiichi">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139891|title=National Astronomical Observatory First Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|Agency for Cultural Affairs]]}}</ref> It was designed by the construction division of [[Tokyo Imperial University]].<ref name="bunka-daiichi" />
The equatorial telescope has a focal length of 359cm and operates using a weight-driven clock mechanism to adjust its speed. The dome is notable for being made of wood.
From [[1938]] to 1999, the telescope was used for solar sunspot observation.<ref name="20cmts" /> The room was registered as a [[Registered Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Registered Tangible Cultural Property]] in 2002.<ref name="20cmts" /><ref name="bunka-daiichi" />
=== Large Equatorial Room === [[File:NAOJ Mitaka Campus 04.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Large Equatorial Room]] Constructed in 1926 and equi11ed with a 65cm refracting telescope in 1929, manufactured by Carl Zeiss.<ref name="hmuseum">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/observatory-history-museum.html|title=Astronomical Observatory History Museum - Large Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The reinforced concrete building has a floor area of 238 square meters.<ref name="bunka-daisekidougi">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187350|title=National Astronomical Observatory Large Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The telescope has a focal length of 1021cm. Due to the large height changes required for observations, the entire floor was designed to rise and fall (range: 360cm) but was fixed in 2000.<ref name="hmuseum" />
The telescope was primarily used for star position observations but ceased operations due to aging. The room, with its wooden dome, was converted into the "Astronomical Observatory History Museum" in 2001 and registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 2002.<ref name="hmuseum" /><ref name="bunka-daisekidougi" />
=== 50cm Public Telescope === A 50cm [[Cassegrain reflector]] telescope built in 1995 by Mitaka Optical Instruments.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/50cm-telescope.html|title=50cm Public Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> It has been in use for public observation sessions twice a month since April 1996.
**Specifications:** - Aperture: 510mm (effective aperture: 500mm) - Focal length: 6030mm - Mount: Fork-type equatorial mount (automated) - Visual limiting magnitude: 16 - CCD limiting magnitude: 21
=== Solar Tower Telescope === [[File:Solar Tower Telescope Einstein tower Mitaka NAOJ 2010.jpg|thumb|180px|Einstein Tower]] Constructed in 1930, it is a reinforced concrete building with five above-ground and one underground floor.<ref name="stower">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/solar-tower-telescope.html|title=Solar Tower Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The facility is modeled after the "Einstein Tower" in [[Potsdam]], Germany.<ref name="stower" /> Registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 1998.<ref name="bunka-taiyoutou">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/147175|title=National Astronomical Observatory Solar Spectroheliograph Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
**Additional Facilities:** - [[Meridian Circles]] - [[TAMA300]] gravitational wave experiment facility - 4D2U Dome Theater - Visitor access and public lectures
== Location == - Address: 2-21-1 Osawa, Mitaka, Tokyo, Japan - Accessibility: By bus from [[Musashi-Sakai Station]] or [[Chofu Station]]

revision.text:
'''National Astronomical Observatory of Japan Mitaka Campus''' (国立天文台三鷹キャンパス) is the headquarters of the [[National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]] (NAOJ), part of the [[National Institutes of Natural Sciences]], located in [[Mitaka]], [[Tokyo]]. The headquarters of the [[Astronomical Society of Japan]] is also located on the campus.
Additionally, the [[Research Center for Astronomy, University of Tokyo]] is situated on this campus. Although it operates as a separate organization, it maintains close relationships with NAOJ through personnel exchanges and collaborative research.
== Facilities ==
=== First Equatorial Room === [[File:The 20-cm Telescope Dome in NAOJ Mitaka Campus-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|First Equatorial Room]] Constructed in [[1921]], a 20cm equatorial telescope manufactured by [[Carl Zeiss AG|Carl Zeiss]] was installed in 1927.<ref name="20cmts">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/20cm-telescope-dome.html|title=First Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The structure is a two-story reinforced concrete building with a floor area of 50 square meters.<ref name="bunka-daiichi">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139891|title=National Astronomical Observatory First Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|Agency for Cultural Affairs]]}}</ref> It was designed by the construction division of [[Tokyo Imperial University]].<ref name="bunka-daiichi" />
The equatorial telescope has a focal length of 359cm and operates using a weight-driven clock mechanism to adjust its speed. The dome is notable for being made of wood.
From [[1938]] to 1999, the telescope was used for solar sunspot observation.<ref name="20cmts" /> The room was registered as a [[Registered Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Registered Tangible Cultural Property]] in 2002.<ref name="20cmts" /><ref name="bunka-daiichi" />
=== Large Equatorial Room === [[File:NAOJ Mitaka Campus 04.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Large Equatorial Room]] Constructed in 1926 and equi11ed with a 65cm refracting telescope in 1929, manufactured by Carl Zeiss.<ref name="hmuseum">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/observatory-history-museum.html|title=Astronomical Observatory History Museum - Large Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The reinforced concrete building has a floor area of 238 square meters.<ref name="bunka-daisekidougi">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187350|title=National Astronomical Observatory Large Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The telescope has a focal length of 1021cm. Due to the large height changes required for observations, the entire floor was designed to rise and fall (range: 360cm) but was fixed in 2000.<ref name="hmuseum" />
The telescope was primarily used for star position observations but ceased operations due to aging. The room, with its wooden dome, was converted into the "Astronomical Observatory History Museum" in 2001 and registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 2002.<ref name="hmuseum" /><ref name="bunka-daisekidougi" />
=== 50cm Public Telescope === A 50cm [[Cassegrain reflector]] telescope built in 1995 by Mitaka Optical Instruments.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/50cm-telescope.html|title=50cm Public Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> It has been in use for public observation sessions twice a month since April 1996.
**Specifications:** - Aperture: 510mm (effective aperture: 500mm) - Focal length: 6030mm - Mount: Fork-type equatorial mount (automated) - Visual limiting magnitude: 16 - CCD limiting magnitude: 21
=== Solar Tower Telescope === [[File:Solar Tower Telescope Einstein tower Mitaka NAOJ 2010.jpg|thumb|180px|Einstein Tower]] Constructed in 1930, it is a reinforced concrete building with five above-ground and one underground floor.<ref name="stower">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/solar-tower-telescope.html|title=Solar Tower Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The facility is modeled after the "Einstein Tower" in [[Potsdam]], Germany.<ref name="stower" /> Registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 1998.<ref name="bunka-taiyoutou">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/147175|title=National Astronomical Observatory Solar Spectroheliograph Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
**Additional Facilities:** - [[Meridian Circles]] - [[TAMA300]] gravitational wave experiment facility - 4D2U Dome Theater - Visitor access and public lectures
== Location == - Address: 2-21-1 Osawa, Mitaka, Tokyo, Japan - Accessibility: By bus from [[Musashi-Sakai Station]] or [[Chofu Station]]== See Also == * [[National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]] * [[Ishigakijima Astronomical Observatory]] * [[Subaru Telescope]]
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == - [http://www.nao.ac.jp National Astronomical Observatory of Japan] - [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00000565 Cultural Heritage Database – Solar Tower Telescope]

'''National Astronomical Observatory of Japan Mitaka Campus''' (国立天文台三鷹キャンパス) is the headquarters of the [[National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]] (NAOJ), part of the [[National Institutes of Natural Sciences]], located in [[Mitaka]], [[Tokyo]]. The headquarters of the [[Astronomical Society of Japan]] is also located on the campus.
Additionally, the [[Research Center for Astronomy, University of Tokyo]] is situated on this campus. Although it operates as a separate organization, it maintains close relationships with NAOJ through personnel exchanges and collaborative research.
== Facilities ==
=== First Equatorial Room === [[File:The 20-cm Telescope Dome in NAOJ Mitaka Campus-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|First Equatorial Room]] Constructed in [[1921]], a 20cm equatorial telescope manufactured by [[Carl Zeiss AG|Carl Zeiss]] was installed in 1927.<ref name="20cmts">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/20cm-telescope-dome.html|title=First Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The structure is a two-story reinforced concrete building with a floor area of 50 square meters.<ref name="bunka-daiichi">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139891|title=National Astronomical Observatory First Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|Agency for Cultural Affairs]]}}</ref> It was designed by the construction division of [[Tokyo Imperial University]].<ref name="bunka-daiichi" />
The equatorial telescope has a focal length of 359cm and operates using a weight-driven clock mechanism to adjust its speed. The dome is notable for being made of wood.
From [[1938]] to 1999, the telescope was used for solar sunspot observation.<ref name="20cmts" /> The room was registered as a [[Registered Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Registered Tangible Cultural Property]] in 2002.<ref name="20cmts" /><ref name="bunka-daiichi" />
=== Large Equatorial Room === [[File:NAOJ Mitaka Campus 04.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Large Equatorial Room]] Constructed in 1926 and equi11ed with a 65cm refracting telescope in 1929, manufactured by Carl Zeiss.<ref name="hmuseum">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/observatory-history-museum.html|title=Astronomical Observatory History Museum - Large Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The reinforced concrete building has a floor area of 238 square meters.<ref name="bunka-daisekidougi">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187350|title=National Astronomical Observatory Large Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The telescope has a focal length of 1021cm. Due to the large height changes required for observations, the entire floor was designed to rise and fall (range: 360cm) but was fixed in 2000.<ref name="hmuseum" />
The telescope was primarily used for star position observations but ceased operations due to aging. The room, with its wooden dome, was converted into the "Astronomical Observatory History Museum" in 2001 and registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 2002.<ref name="hmuseum" /><ref name="bunka-daisekidougi" />
=== 50cm Public Telescope === A 50cm [[Cassegrain reflector]] telescope built in 1995 by Mitaka Optical Instruments.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/50cm-telescope.html|title=50cm Public Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> It has been in use for public observation sessions twice a month since April 1996.
**Specifications:** - Aperture: 510mm (effective aperture: 500mm) - Focal length: 6030mm - Mount: Fork-type equatorial mount (automated) - Visual limiting magnitude: 16 - CCD limiting magnitude: 21
=== Solar Tower Telescope === [[File:Solar Tower Telescope Einstein tower Mitaka NAOJ 2010.jpg|thumb|180px|Einstein Tower]] Constructed in 1930, it is a reinforced concrete building with five above-ground and one underground floor.<ref name="stower">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/solar-tower-telescope.html|title=Solar Tower Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The facility is modeled after the "Einstein Tower" in [[Potsdam]], Germany.<ref name="stower" /> Registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 1998.<ref name="bunka-taiyoutou">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/147175|title=National Astronomical Observatory Solar Spectroheliograph Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
**Additional Facilities:** - [[Meridian Circles]] - [[TAMA300]] gravitational wave experiment facility - 4D2U Dome Theater - Visitor access and public lectures
== Location == - Address: 2-21-1 Osawa, Mitaka, Tokyo, Japan - Accessibility: By bus from [[Musashi-Sakai Station]] or [[Chofu Station]]== See Also == * [[National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]] * [[Ishigakijima Astronomical Observatory]] * [[Subaru Telescope]]

=== 50cm Public Telescope === A 50cm [[Cassegrain reflector]] telescope built in 1995 by Mitaka Optical Instruments.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/50cm-telescope.html|title=50cm Public Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> It has been in use for public observation sessions twice a month since April 1996.
**Specifications:** - Aperture: 510mm (effective aperture: 500mm) - Focal length: 6030mm - Mount: Fork-type equatorial mount (automated) - Visual limiting magnitude: 16 - CCD limiting magnitude: 21
=== Solar Tower Telescope === [[File:Solar Tower Telescope Einstein tower Mitaka NAOJ 2010.jpg|thumb|180px|Einstein Tower]] Constructed in 1930, it is a reinforced concrete building with five above-ground and one underground floor.<ref name="stower">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/solar-tower-telescope.html|title=Solar Tower Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The facility is modeled after the "Einstein Tower" in [[Potsdam]], Germany.<ref name="stower" /> Registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 1998.<ref name="bunka-taiyoutou">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/147175|title=National Astronomical Observatory Solar Spectroheliograph Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
**Additional Facilities:** - [[Meridian Circles]] - [[TAMA300]] gravitational wave experiment facility - 4D2U Dome Theater - Visitor access and public lectures
== Location == - Address: 2-21-1 Osawa, Mitaka, Tokyo, Japan - Accessibility: By bus from [[Musashi-Sakai Station]] or [[Chofu Station]] == See Also! == * [[National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]] * [[Ishigakijima Astronomical Observatory]] * [[Subaru Telescope]]
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == - [http://www.nao.ac.jp National Astronomical Observatory of Japan] - [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00000565 Cultural Heritage Database – Solar Tower Telescope]

revision.text:
'''National Astronomical Observatory of Japan Mitaka Campus''' (国立天文台三鷹キャンパス) is the headquarters of the [[National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]] (NAOJ), part of the [[National Institutes of Natural Sciences]], located in [[Mitaka]], [[Tokyo]]. The headquarters of the [[Astronomical Society of Japan]] is also located on the campus.
Additionally, the [[Research Center for Astronomy, University of Tokyo]] is situated on this campus. Although it operates as a separate organization, it maintains close relationships with NAOJ through personnel exchanges and collaborative research.
== Facilities ==
=== First Equatorial Room === [[File:The 20-cm Telescope Dome in NAOJ Mitaka Campus-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|First Equatorial Room]] Constructed in [[1921]], a 20cm equatorial telescope manufactured by [[Carl Zeiss AG|Carl Zeiss]] was installed in 1927.<ref name="20cmts">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/20cm-telescope-dome.html|title=First Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The structure is a two-story reinforced concrete building with a floor area of 50 square meters.<ref name="bunka-daiichi">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139891|title=National Astronomical Observatory First Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|Agency for Cultural Affairs]]}}</ref> It was designed by the construction division of [[Tokyo Imperial University]].<ref name="bunka-daiichi" />
The equatorial telescope has a focal length of 359cm and operates using a weight-driven clock mechanism to adjust its speed. The dome is notable for being made of wood.
From [[1938]] to 1999, the telescope was used for solar sunspot observation.<ref name="20cmts" /> The room was registered as a [[Registered Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Registered Tangible Cultural Property]] in 2002.<ref name="20cmts" /><ref name="bunka-daiichi" />
=== Large Equatorial Room === [[File:NAOJ Mitaka Campus 04.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Large Equatorial Room]] Constructed in 1926 and equi11ed with a 65cm refracting telescope in 1929, manufactured by Carl Zeiss.<ref name="hmuseum">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/observatory-history-museum.html|title=Astronomical Observatory History Museum - Large Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The reinforced concrete building has a floor area of 238 square meters.<ref name="bunka-daisekidougi">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187350|title=National Astronomical Observatory Large Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The telescope has a focal length of 1021cm. Due to the large height changes required for observations, the entire floor was designed to rise and fall (range: 360cm) but was fixed in 2000.<ref name="hmuseum" />
The telescope was primarily used for star position observations but ceased operations due to aging. The room, with its wooden dome, was converted into the "Astronomical Observatory History Museum" in 2001 and registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 2002.<ref name="hmuseum" /><ref name="bunka-daisekidougi" />
=== 50cm Public Telescope === A 50cm [[Cassegrain reflector]] telescope built in 1995 by Mitaka Optical Instruments.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/50cm-telescope.html|title=50cm Public Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> It has been in use for public observation sessions twice a month since April 1996.
**Specifications:** - Aperture: 510mm (effective aperture: 500mm) - Focal length: 6030mm - Mount: Fork-type equatorial mount (automated) - Visual limiting magnitude: 16 - CCD limiting magnitude: 21
=== Solar Tower Telescope === [[File:Solar Tower Telescope Einstein tower Mitaka NAOJ 2010.jpg|thumb|180px|Einstein Tower]] Constructed in 1930, it is a reinforced concrete building with five above-ground and one underground floor.<ref name="stower">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/solar-tower-telescope.html|title=Solar Tower Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The facility is modeled after the "Einstein Tower" in [[Potsdam]], Germany.<ref name="stower" /> Registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 1998.<ref name="bunka-taiyoutou">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/147175|title=National Astronomical Observatory Solar Spectroheliograph Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
**Additional Facilities:** - [[Meridian Circles]] - [[TAMA300]] gravitational wave experiment facility - 4D2U Dome Theater - Visitor access and public lectures
== Location == - Address: 2-21-1 Osawa, Mitaka, Tokyo, Japan - Accessibility: By bus from [[Musashi-Sakai Station]] or [[Chofu Station]] == See Also == * [[National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]] * [[Ishigakijima Astronomical Observatory]] * [[Subaru Telescope]]
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == - [http://www.nao.ac.jp National Astronomical Observatory of Japan] - [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00000565 Cultural Heritage Database – Solar Tower Telescope]

'''National Astronomical Observatory of Japan Mitaka Campus''' (国立天文台三鷹キャンパス) is the headquarters of the [[National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]] (NAOJ), part of the [[National Institutes of Natural Sciences]], located in [[Mitaka]], [[Tokyo]]. The headquarters of the [[Astronomical Society of Japan]] is also located on the campus.
Additionally, the [[Research Center for Astronomy, University of Tokyo]] is situated on this campus. Although it operates as a separate organization, it maintains close relationships with NAOJ through personnel exchanges and collaborative research.

=== First Equatorial Room === [[File:The 20-cm Telescope Dome in NAOJ Mitaka Campus-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|First Equatorial Room]] Constructed in [[1921]], a 20cm equatorial telescope manufactured by [[Carl Zeiss AG|Carl Zeiss]] was installed in 1927.<ref name="20cmts">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/20cm-telescope-dome.html|title=First Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The structure is a two-story reinforced concrete building with a floor area of 50 square meters.<ref name="bunka-daiichi">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139891|title=National Astronomical Observatory First Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|Agency for Cultural Affairs]]}}</ref> It was designed by the construction division of [[Tokyo Imperial University]].<ref name="bunka-daiichi" />

The telescope was primarily used for star position observations but ceased operations due to aging. The room, with its wooden dome, was converted into the "Astronomical Observatory History Museum" in 2001 and registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 2002.<ref name="hmuseum" /><ref name="bunka-daisekidougi" />
=== 50cm Public Telescope === A 50cm [[Cassegrain reflector]] telescope built in 1995 by Mitaka Optical Instruments.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/50cm-telescope.html|title=50cm Public Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> It has been in use for public observation sessions twice a month since April 1996.
**Specifications:** - Aperture: 510mm (effective aperture: 500mm) - Focal length: 6030mm - Mount: Fork-type equatorial mount (automated) - Visual limiting magnitude: 16 - CCD limiting magnitude: 21
=== Solar Tower Telescope === [[File:Solar Tower Telescope Einstein tower Mitaka NAOJ 2010.jpg|thumb|180px|Einstein Tower]] Constructed in 1930, it is a reinforced concrete building with five above-ground and one underground floor.<ref name="stower">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/solar-tower-telescope.html|title=Solar Tower Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The facility is modeled after the "Einstein Tower" in [[Potsdam]], Germany.<ref name="stower" /> Registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 1998.<ref name="bunka-taiyoutou">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/147175|title=National Astronomical Observatory Solar Spectroheliograph Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
**Additional Facilities:** - [[Meridian Circles]] - [[TAMA300]] gravitational wave experiment facility - 4D2U Dome Theater - Visitor access and public lectures
== Location == - Address: 2-21-1 Osawa, Mitaka, Tokyo, Japan - Accessibility: By bus from [[Musashi-Sakai Station]] or [[Chofu Station]] == See Also == * [[National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]] * [[Ishigakijima Astronomical Observatory]] * [[Subaru Telescope]]

'''National Astronomical Observatory of Japan Mitaka Campus''' (国立天文台三鷹キャンパス) is the headquarters of the [[National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]] (NAOJ), part of the [[National Institutes of Natural Sciences]], located in [[Mitaka]], [[Tokyo]]. The headquarters of the [[Astronomical Society of Japan]] is also located on the campus.
Additionally, the [[Research Center for Astronomy, University of Tokyo]] is situated on this campus. Although it operates as a separate organization, it maintains close relationships with NAOJ through personnel exchanges and collaborative research.
== Facilities ==
=== First Equatorial Room === [[File:The 20-cm Telescope Dome in NAOJ Mitaka Campus-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|First Equatorial Room]] Constructed in [[1921]], a 20cm equatorial telescope manufactured by [[Carl Zeiss AG|Carl Zeiss]] was installed in 1927.<ref name="20cmts">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/20cm-telescope-dome.html|title=First Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The structure is a two-story reinforced concrete building with a floor area of 50 square meters.<ref name="bunka-daiichi">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139891|title=National Astronomical Observatory First Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|Agency for Cultural Affairs]]}}</ref> It was designed by the construction division of [[Tokyo Imperial University]].<ref name="bunka-daiichi" />
The equatorial telescope has a focal length of 359cm and operates using a weight-driven clock mechanism to adjust its speed. The dome is notable for being made of wood.
From [[1938]] to 1999, the telescope was used for solar sunspot observation.<ref name="20cmts" /> The room was registered as a [[Registered Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Registered Tangible Cultural Property]] in 2002.<ref name="20cmts" /><ref name="bunka-daiichi" />
=== Large Equatorial Room === [[File:NAOJ Mitaka Campus 04.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Large Equatorial Room]] Constructed in 1926 and equi11ed with a 65cm refracting telescope in 1929, manufactured by Carl Zeiss.<ref name="hmuseum">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/observatory-history-museum.html|title=Astronomical Observatory History Museum - Large Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The reinforced concrete building has a floor area of 238 square meters.<ref name="bunka-daisekidougi">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187350|title=National Astronomical Observatory Large Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The telescope has a focal length of 1021cm. Due to the large height changes required for observations, the entire floor was designed to rise and fall (range: 360cm) but was fixed in 2000.<ref name="hmuseum" />
The telescope was primarily used for star position observations but ceased operations due to aging. The room, with its wooden dome, was converted into the "Astronomical Observatory History Museum" in 2001 and registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 2002.<ref name="hmuseum" /><ref name="bunka-daisekidougi" />
=== 50cm Public Telescope === A 50cm [[Cassegrain reflector]] telescope built in 1995 by Mitaka Optical Instruments.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/50cm-telescope.html|title=50cm Public Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> It has been in use for public observation sessions twice a month since April 1996.
**Specifications:** - Aperture: 510mm (effective aperture: 500mm) - Focal length: 6030mm - Mount: Fork-type equatorial mount (automated) - Visual limiting magnitude: 16 - CCD limiting magnitude: 21
=== Solar Tower Telescope === [[File:Solar Tower Telescope Einstein tower Mitaka NAOJ 2010.jpg|thumb|180px|Einstein Tower]] Constructed in 1930, it is a reinforced concrete building with five above-ground and one underground floor.<ref name="stower">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/solar-tower-telescope.html|title=Solar Tower Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The facility is modeled after the "Einstein Tower" in [[Potsdam]], Germany.<ref name="stower" /> Registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 1998.<ref name="bunka-taiyoutou">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/147175|title=National Astronomical Observatory Solar Spectroheliograph Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
**Additional Facilities:** - [[Meridian Circles]] - [[TAMA300]] gravitational wave experiment facility - 4D2U Dome Theater - Visitor access and public lectures
== Location == - Address: 2-21-1 Osawa, Mitaka, Tokyo, Japan - Accessibility: By bus from [[Musashi-Sakai Station]] or [[Chofu Station]] == See Also == * [[National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]] * [[Ishigakijima Astronomical Observatory]] * [[Subaru Telescope]]
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == - [http://www.nao.ac.jp National Astronomical Observatory of Japan] - [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00000565 Cultural Heritage Database – Solar Tower Telescope]

'''National Astronomical Observatory of Japan Mitaka Campus''' (国立天文台三鷹キャンパス) is the headquarters of the [[National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]] (NAOJ), part of the [[National Institutes of Natural Sciences]], located in [[Mitaka]], [[Tokyo]]. The headquarters of the [[Astronomical Society of Japan]] is also located on the campus.
Additionally, the [[Research Center for Astronomy, University of Tokyo]] is situated on this campus. Although it operates as a separate organization, it maintains close relationships with NAOJ through personnel exchanges and collaborative research.
== Facilities ==
=== First Equatorial Room === [[File:The 20-cm Telescope Dome in NAOJ Mitaka Campus-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|First Equatorial Room]] Constructed in [[1921]], a 20cm equatorial telescope manufactured by [[Carl Zeiss AG|Carl Zeiss]] was installed in 1927.<ref name="20cmts">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/20cm-telescope-dome.html|title=First Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The structure is a two-story reinforced concrete building with a floor area of 50 square meters.<ref name="bunka-daiichi">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139891|title=National Astronomical Observatory First Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|Agency for Cultural Affairs]]}}</ref> It was designed by the construction division of [[Tokyo Imperial University]].<ref name="bunka-daiichi" />
The equatorial telescope has a focal length of 359cm and operates using a weight-driven clock mechanism to adjust its speed. The dome is notable for being made of wood.
From [[1938]] to 1999, the telescope was used for solar sunspot observation.<ref name="20cmts" /> The room was registered as a [[Registered Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Registered Tangible Cultural Property]] in 2002.<ref name="20cmts" /><ref name="bunka-daiichi" />
=== Large Equatorial Room === [[File:NAOJ Mitaka Campus 04.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Large Equatorial Room]] Constructed in 1926 and equi11ed with a 65cm refracting telescope in 1929, manufactured by Carl Zeiss.<ref name="hmuseum">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/observatory-history-museum.html|title=Astronomical Observatory History Museum - Large Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The reinforced concrete building has a floor area of 238 square meters.<ref name="bunka-daisekidougi">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187350|title=National Astronomical Observatory Large Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The telescope has a focal length of 1021cm. Due to the large height changes required for observations, the entire floor was designed to rise and fall (range: 360cm) but was fixed in 2000.<ref name="hmuseum" />
The telescope was primarily used for star position observations but ceased operations due to aging. The room, with its wooden dome, was converted into the "Astronomical Observatory History Museum" in 2001 and registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 2002.<ref name="hmuseum" /><ref name="bunka-daisekidougi" />
=== 50cm Public Telescope === A 50cm [[Cassegrain reflector]] telescope built in 1995 by Mitaka Optical Instruments.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/50cm-telescope.html|title=50cm Public Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> It has been in use for public observation sessions twice a month since April 1996.
**Specifications:** - Aperture: 510mm (effective aperture: 500mm) - Focal length: 6030mm - Mount: Fork-type equatorial mount (automated) - Visual limiting magnitude: 16 - CCD limiting magnitude: 21
=== Solar Tower Telescope === [[File:Solar Tower Telescope Einstein tower Mitaka NAOJ 2010.jpg|thumb|180px|Einstein Tower]] Constructed in 1930, it is a reinforced concrete building with five above-ground and one underground floor.<ref name="stower">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/solar-tower-telescope.html|title=Solar Tower Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The facility is modeled after the "Einstein Tower" in [[Potsdam]], Germany.<ref name="stower" /> Registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 1998.<ref name="bunka-taiyoutou">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/147175|title=National Astronomical Observatory Solar Spectroheliograph Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
**Additional Facilities:** - [[Meridian Circles]] - [[TAMA300]] gravitational wave experiment facility - 4D2U Dome Theater - Visitor access and public lectures
== Location == - Address: 2-21-1 Osawa, Mitaka, Tokyo, Japan - Accessibility: By bus from [[Musashi-Sakai Station]] or [[Chofu Station]] == See Also == * [[National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]] * [[Ishigakijima Astronomical Observatory]] * [[Subaru Telescope]]

'''National Astronomical Observatory of Japan Mitaka Campus''' (国立天文台三鷹キャンパス) is the headquarters of the [[National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]] (NAOJ), part of the [[National Institutes of Natural Sciences]], located in [[Mitaka]], [[Tokyo]]. The headquarters of the [[Astronomical Society of Japan]] is also located on the campus.
Additionally, the [[Research Center for Astronomy, University of Tokyo]] is situated on this campus. Although it operates as a separate organization, it maintains close relationships with NAOJ through personnel exchanges and collaborative research.
== Facilities ==
=== First Equatorial Room === [[File:The 20-cm Telescope Dome in NAOJ Mitaka Campus-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|First Equatorial Room]] Constructed in [[1921]], a 20cm equatorial telescope manufactured by [[Carl Zeiss AG|Carl Zeiss]] was installed in 1927.<ref name="20cmts">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/20cm-telescope-dome.html|title=First Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The structure is a two-story reinforced concrete building with a floor area of 50 square meters.<ref name="bunka-daiichi">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139891|title=National Astronomical Observatory First Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|Agency for Cultural Affairs]]}}</ref> It was designed by the construction division of [[Tokyo Imperial University]].<ref name="bunka-daiichi" />
The equatorial telescope has a focal length of 359cm and operates using a weight-driven clock mechanism to adjust its speed. The dome is notable for being made of wood.
From [[1938]] to 1999, the telescope was used for solar sunspot observation.<ref name="20cmts" /> The room was registered as a [[Registered Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Registered Tangible Cultural Property]] in 2002.<ref name="20cmts" /><ref name="bunka-daiichi" />
=== Large Equatorial Room === [[File:NAOJ Mitaka Campus 04.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Large Equatorial Room]] Constructed in 1926 and equi11ed with a 65cm refracting telescope in 1929, manufactured by Carl Zeiss.<ref name="hmuseum">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/observatory-history-museum.html|title=Astronomical Observatory History Museum - Large Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The reinforced concrete building has a floor area of 238 square meters.<ref name="bunka-daisekidougi">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187350|title=National Astronomical Observatory Large Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The telescope has a focal length of 1021cm. Due to the large height changes required for observations, the entire floor was designed to rise and fall (range: 360cm) but was fixed in 2000.<ref name="hmuseum" />
The telescope was primarily used for star position observations but ceased operations due to aging. The room, with its wooden dome, was converted into the "Astronomical Observatory History Museum" in 2001 and registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 2002.<ref name="hmuseum" /><ref name="bunka-daisekidougi" />
=== 50cm Public Telescope === A 50cm [[Cassegrain reflector]] telescope built in 1995 by Mitaka Optical Instruments.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/50cm-telescope.html|title=50cm Public Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> It has been in use for public observation sessions twice a month since April 1996.
**Specifications:** - Aperture: 510mm (effective aperture: 500mm) - Focal length: 6030mm - Mount: Fork-type equatorial mount (automated) - Visual limiting magnitude: 16 - CCD limiting magnitude: 21
=== Solar Tower Telescope === [[File:Solar Tower Telescope Einstein tower Mitaka NAOJ 2010.jpg|thumb|180px|Einstein Tower]] Constructed in 1930, it is a reinforced concrete building with five above-ground and one underground floor.<ref name="stower">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/solar-tower-telescope.html|title=Solar Tower Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The facility is modeled after the "Einstein Tower" in [[Potsdam]], Germany.<ref name="stower" /> Registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 1998.<ref name="bunka-taiyoutou">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/147175|title=National Astronomical Observatory Solar Spectroheliograph Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
**Additional Facilities:** - [[Meridian Circles]] - [[TAMA300]] gravitational wave experiment facility - 4D2U Dome Theater - Visitor access and public lectures
== Location == - Address: 2-21-1 Osawa, Mitaka, Tokyo, Japan - Accessibility: By bus from [[Musashi-Sakai Station]] or [[Chofu Station]] == See Also == * [[National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]] * [[Ishigakijima Astronomical Observatory]] * [[Subaru Telescope]]
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == - [http://www.nao.ac.jp National Astronomical Observatory of Japan] - [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00000565 Cultural Heritage Database – Solar Tower Telescope]

revision.text:
'''National Astronomical Observatory of Japan Mitaka Campus''' (国立天文台三鷹キャンパス) is the headquarters of the [[National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]] (NAOJ), part of the [[National Institutes of Natural Sciences]], located in [[Mitaka]], [[Tokyo]]. The headquarters of the [[Astronomical Society of Japan]] is also located on the campus.
Additionally, the [[Research Center for Astronomy, University of Tokyo]] is situated on this campus. Although it operates as a separate organization, it maintains close relationships with NAOJ through personnel exchanges and collaborative research.
== Facilities ==
=== First Equatorial Room === [[File:The 20-cm Telescope Dome in NAOJ Mitaka Campus-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|First Equatorial Room]] Constructed in [[1921]], a 20cm equatorial telescope manufactured by [[Carl Zeiss AG|Carl Zeiss]] was installed in 1927.<ref name="20cmts">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/20cm-telescope-dome.html|title=First Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The structure is a two-story reinforced concrete building with a floor area of 50 square meters.<ref name="bunka-daiichi">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139891|title=National Astronomical Observatory First Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|Agency for Cultural Affairs]]}}</ref> It was designed by the construction division of [[Tokyo Imperial University]].<ref name="bunka-daiichi" />
The equatorial telescope has a focal length of 359cm and operates using a weight-driven clock mechanism to adjust its speed. The dome is notable for being made of wood.
From [[1938]] to 1999, the telescope was used for solar sunspot observation.<ref name="20cmts" /> The room was registered as a [[Registered Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Registered Tangible Cultural Property]] in 2002.<ref name="20cmts" /><ref name="bunka-daiichi" />
=== Large Equatorial Room === [[File:NAOJ Mitaka Campus 04.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Large Equatorial Room]] Constructed in 1926 and equi11ed with a 65cm refracting telescope in 1929, manufactured by Carl Zeiss.<ref name="hmuseum">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/observatory-history-museum.html|title=Astronomical Observatory History Museum - Large Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The reinforced concrete building has a floor area of 238 square meters.<ref name="bunka-daisekidougi">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187350|title=National Astronomical Observatory Large Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The telescope has a focal length of 1021cm. Due to the large height changes required for observations, the entire floor was designed to rise and fall (range: 360cm) but was fixed in 2000.<ref name="hmuseum" />
The telescope was primarily used for star position observations but ceased operations due to aging. The room, with its wooden dome, was converted into the "Astronomical Observatory History Museum" in 2001 and registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 2002.<ref name="hmuseum" /><ref name="bunka-daisekidougi" />
=== 50cm Public Telescope === A 50cm [[Cassegrain reflector]] telescope built in 1995 by Mitaka Optical Instruments.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/50cm-telescope.html|title=50cm Public Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> It has been in use for public observation sessions twice a month since April 1996.
**Specifications:** - Aperture: 510mm (effective aperture: 500mm) - Focal length: 6030mm - Mount: Fork-type equatorial mount (automated) - Visual limiting magnitude: 16 - CCD limiting magnitude: 21
=== Solar Tower Telescope === [[File:Solar Tower Telescope Einstein tower Mitaka NAOJ 2010.jpg|thumb|180px|Einstein Tower]] Constructed in 1930, it is a reinforced concrete building with five above-ground and one underground floor.<ref name="stower">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/solar-tower-telescope.html|title=Solar Tower Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The facility is modeled after the "Einstein Tower" in [[Potsdam]], Germany.<ref name="stower" /> Registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 1998.<ref name="bunka-taiyoutou">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/147175|title=National Astronomical Observatory Solar Spectroheliograph Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
**Additional Facilities:** - [[Meridian Circles]] - [[TAMA300]] gravitational wave experiment facility - 4D2U Dome Theater - Visitor access and public lectures
== Location == - Address: 2-21-1 Osawa, Mitaka, Tokyo, Japan - Accessibility: By bus from [[Musashi-Sakai Station]] or [[Chofu Station]] == See Also == * [[National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]] * [[Ishigakijima Astronomical Observatory]] * [[Subaru Telescope]]
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == - [http://www.nao.ac.jp National Astronomical Observatory of Japan] - [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00000565 Cultural Heritage Database – Solar Tower Telescope]

=== Large Equatorial Room === [[File:NAOJ Mitaka Campus 04.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Large Equatorial Room]] Constructed in 1926 and equi11ed with a 65cm refracting telescope in 1929, manufactured by Carl Zeiss.<ref name="hmuseum">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/observatory-history-museum.html|title=Astronomical Observatory History Museum - Large Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The reinforced concrete building has a floor area of 238 square meters.<ref name="bunka-daisekidougi">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187350|title=National Astronomical Observatory Large Equatorial Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>

**Specifications:** - Aperture: 510mm (effective aperture: 500mm) - Focal length: 6030mm - Mount: Fork-type equatorial mount (automated) - Visual limiting magnitude: 16 - CCD limiting magnitude: 21
=== Solar Tower Telescope === [[File:Solar Tower Telescope Einstein tower Mitaka NAOJ 2010.jpg|thumb|180px|Einstein Tower]] Constructed in 1930, it is a reinforced concrete building with five above-ground and one underground floor.<ref name="stower">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/solar-tower-telescope.html|title=Solar Tower Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The facility is modeled after the "Einstein Tower" in [[Potsdam]], Germany.<ref name="stower" /> Registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 1998.<ref name="bunka-taiyoutou">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/147175|title=National Astronomical Observatory Solar Spectroheliograph Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>

The room, with its wooden dome, was converted into the "Astronomical Observatory History Museum" in 2001 and registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 2002.<ref name="hmuseum" /><ref name="bunka-daisekidougi" />
=== 50cm Public Telescope === A 50cm [[Cassegrain reflector]] telescope built in 1995 by Mitaka Optical Instruments.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/50cm-telescope.html|title=50cm Public Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> It has been in use for public observation sessions twice a month since April 1996.
**Specifications:** - Aperture: 510mm (effective aperture: 500mm) - Focal length: 6030mm - Mount: Fork-type equatorial mount (automated) - Visual limiting magnitude: 16 - CCD limiting magnitude: 21
=== Solar Tower Telescope === [[File:Solar Tower Telescope Einstein tower Mitaka NAOJ 2010.jpg|thumb|180px|Einstein Tower]] Constructed in 1930, it is a reinforced concrete building with five above-ground and one underground floor.<ref name="stower">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nao.ac.jp/access/mitaka/facilities/solar-tower-telescope.html|title=Solar Tower Telescope|accessdate=2018-04-12|publisher=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}}</ref> The facility is modeled after the "Einstein Tower" in [[Potsdam]], Germany.<ref name="stower" /> Registered as a Registered Tangible Cultural Property in 1998.<ref name="bunka-taiyoutou">{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/147175|title=National Astronomical Observatory Solar Spectroheliograph Room|accessdate=2018-06-29|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
**Additional Facilities:** - [[Meridian Circles]] - [[TAMA300]] gravitational wave experiment facility - 4D2U Dome Theater - Visitor access and public lectures
== Location == - Address: 2-21-1 Osawa, Mitaka, Tokyo, Japan - Accessibility: By bus from [[Musashi-Sakai Station]] or [[Chofu Station]] == See Also == * [[National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]] * [[Ishigakijima Astronomical Observatory]] * [[Subaru Telescope]]
